assertions.expectation: Recommends combining release channels with maintenance exclusions as the approach for maximum control
assertions.reason: The response clearly recommends using Regular channel with maintenance windows and shows extensive use of maintenanceExclusions for different scenarios (month-end, quarter-end, year-end freezes)

assertions.expectation: Recommends 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion to prevent disruptive changes while allowing control plane security patches. May also mention persistent exclusions with --add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support (no longer a 6-month max, auto-tracks EoS)
assertions.reason: The response shows maintenance exclusions with scope: UPGRADES but doesn't specifically recommend 'no minor or node upgrades' exclusion or mention --add-maintenance-exclusion-until-end-of-support functionality

assertions.expectation: Recommends maintenance windows to control WHEN patches are applied (e.g., weekend windows only)
assertions.reason: The response explicitly configures maintenance windows with specific times (Sunday 2-6 AM) and shows both YAML and gcloud command examples for setting maintenance windows

assertions.expectation: Mentions the cluster disruption budget (disruption interval) to control upgrade frequency — patch interval default 7d (max 90d), minor interval default 30d (max 90d)
assertions.reason: The response does not mention cluster disruption budget or disruption intervals for controlling upgrade frequency

assertions.expectation: Suggests Stable or Extended channel for production clusters in regulated environments. Extended channel provides maximum flexibility around EoS enforcement (additional cost only during extended period)
assertions.reason: The response recommends Regular channel instead of Stable or Extended channel, and doesn't mention Extended channel's EoS flexibility or associated costs

assertions.expectation: Notes that disabling control plane patches entirely is not recommended due to security risks — monthly patches at minimum
assertions.reason: The response doesn't explicitly state that disabling control plane patches entirely is not recommended or mention monthly patches as a minimum

assertions.expectation: May mention rollout sequencing as an advanced option for controlling upgrade order across multiple clusters, and/or the cluster disruption budget for controlling CP patch frequency
assertions.reason: The response doesn't mention rollout sequencing for multiple clusters or cluster disruption budget for controlling control plane patch frequency

assertions.expectation: Provides gcloud commands for configuring the recommended exclusion type and maintenance window
assertions.reason: The response provides multiple gcloud commands for configuring maintenance windows and shows examples of maintenance exclusions, though not specifically the recommended exclusion type
